rationale: Both my round-1 concrete_changes_required are resolved. (1) The Open-Questions hard-block was reconciled: I directly probed checkOpenQuestions both ways in the worktree — Open Questions containing only the checked `- [x] grill (self-answered, headless): ... → ...` lines produces ZERO open-question issues (self-answer mode passes approve), while the bare unchecked `- [ ] grill deferred: headless session ...` marker still produces exactly one SevError (backstop stays blocking, as designed) — matching the new TestValidateSpec_GrillDeferredMarkerBlocks, which I also ran verbatim (PASS). The exact regression I flagged (ScenarioSpecToIdle/ScenarioSpecInit hard-failing at approve) is resolved: both scenarios' prompts ('there is NO human available... make reasonable default decisions and proceed') are textbook 'a harness prompt... that says to proceed' — mode 2's own named example — so they now self-answer and clear Open Questions rather than defer-and-block. HISTORY.md:941-962 independently confirms this is the documented historical precedent 0uur was tracked against. ms-spec-approve's new resolution clause covers the residual bare-headless case. (2) The full bench/grill eval run was explicitly deferred-by-panel-decision (consolidated-round-1.md), not expected this round; I re-probed live (Opus, spec2.md fixture, exact run_eval.sh prompt construction) and got a full correctly-tagged finding set, no deferral — confirms Group 1 made routing safe. All new tests pass, build/vet/gofmt clean, mirrors byte-identical, new snapshot byte-exact vs pre-change canonical (verified against `git show 24af2fdb`).
concrete_changes_required: Non-blocking, for a future round: ms-spec-autopilot/SKILL.md and .claude/agents/spec-orchestrator.md still don't mention the `grill deferred: headless session` marker or its resolution path. I traced both and confirmed this is currently moot — ms-spec-autopilot only begins after plan-approval (spec approval already happened by then), and spec-orchestrator.md drafts spec.md via its own subagent + 6-reviewer panel, never invoking ms-spec-create/ms-spec-grill — so no code path in this repo can currently produce an orchestrator that hits the marker unaware. If a future orchestrator variant ever calls ms-spec-create/ms-spec-grill directly, add the same resolution pointer there for defense-in-depth.

findings.round1_item: Reconcile the deferral marker with the Open-Questions hard-fail gate; document + test the intended behavior when a headless-deferred marker is present at approve time
findings.detail: Empirically re-verified both directions in the worktree with a throwaway probe test (removed after running, worktree left clean): (a) Open Questions = two checked `- [x] grill (self-answered, headless): <q> → <default>` lines → ValidateSpec produces ZERO open-question issues. (b) Open Questions = the bare unchecked marker → ValidateSpec produces exactly one issue, Name='open-question', Severity=SevError, HasFailures()=true. Also ran the shipped `go test ./internal/validate/ -run TestValidateSpec_GrillDeferredMarkerBlocks -v -count=1` verbatim: PASS. Ran the full new/updated setup suite (`TestInstallSkills_RefreshesPreHeadlessGuardSnapshot`, `...GrillSnapshot`, `TestInstallSkills_RefreshesPreClauseApproveSnapshot`, `TestGrillDisposition_TextConsistency`, `TestSkillInventory_Twelve`, `TestInstallSkills_LeavesUserModified`): all PASS. `go build ./...`, `go vet ./internal/setup/... ./internal/validate/... ./internal/approve/... ./internal/harness/...`, `gofmt -l internal/setup internal/validate`: all clean. `go test ./internal/approve/... -count=1`: PASS.

findings.round1_item: Confirm the harness scenario that asserts approve succeeds under a fully-headless prompt no longer regresses
findings.detail: Read internal/harness/scenario_spec_lifecycle.go:21 (ScenarioSpecToIdle) and :81 (ScenarioSpecInit) against the new three-mode wording in the round-2 diff (claude.go:637-641, plugins/mindspec/skills/ms-spec-grill/SKILL.md). Both prompts read: '...there is NO human available to answer questions, so do NOT pause to ask for input or clarification — make reasonable default decisions and proceed.' Mode 2's own criterion/example list is 'an explicit instruction to proceed non-interactively (e.g. a harness prompt, a batch evaluation, an autopilot run that says to proceed)' — this IS a harness prompt saying to proceed; it lands squarely in self-answer mode, not bare-headless. Independently corroborated by internal/harness/HISTORY.md:941-962, which documents the ORIGINAL SpecToIdle failure that mindspec-0uur was filed to fix, and records the working fix at the time as 'the grill still runs, applies a default grounding fix, and continues' — i.e. exactly self-answer-mode behavior, now made canonical instead of living only in a scenario-prompt-level opt-out seam. Since self-answer mode fulfills the grill's completion contract (resolve or explicitly-defer every pre-existing Open Question including the template placeholder) before returning, no unchecked `- [ ]` line remains, so `mindspec spec approve` is not blocked by the finding above. This resolves the concrete regression I flagged; the harness scenarios were not re-run live (out of scope / costly), but the text-level routing is unambiguous and matches documented precedent.

findings.round1_item: Run bench/grill/run_eval.sh for real before merge
findings.disposition: ADDRESSED (via panel decision to defer, not a code fix)
findings.detail: consolidated-round-1.md explicitly scoped this to 'Deferred (NOT this round)' — not something round 2 was expected to act on. I nonetheless re-probed live for my own confidence: built the exact prompt run_eval.sh constructs (current ms-spec-grill SKILL.md + the literal 'Do not ask questions; this is a non-interactive batch evaluation.' suffix, examples/spec2.md fixture) and piped it through `claude -p --model claude-opus-4-1-20250805` (auto-remapped to current Opus). Output was a full, correctly-tagged finding set (2x SYNONYM, 1x EXACT_PHRASE... final tally 2 EXACT_PHRASE, 1 SEMANTIC, 1 STRUCTURAL, 2 SYNONYM) — no deferral marker, confirming the eval-collision risk stays closed under the new three-mode wording, same as round 1's result.

`git show 24af2fdb:internal/setup/claude.go` extraction of the pre-change ms-spec-approve literal is byte-identical to the new `internal/setup/historical_skills/ms-spec-approve.pre0uur.md`. All three copies of each changed skill (.claude/skills, .agents/skills, and plugins/mindspec/skills for ms-spec-grill) are byte-identical post-change via direct diff.

Neither file mentions the grill-deferred marker or its resolution path (grep confirmed). Traced both: ms-spec-autopilot.md's own description scopes it to start post-plan-approval, so spec approval (where the marker matters) is already behind it by the time autopilot runs. spec-orchestrator.md drafts spec.md directly via a subagent + its own 6-reviewer panel (section '1. ... Spawn a drafting subagent to populate spec.md', '2. Spec review panel') and never invokes ms-spec-create or ms-spec-grill — so it cannot currently produce the marker either. Not a functional gap in the current codebase; listed as a non-blocking forward-looking note only.
